_meta.purpose: JSON-style templates of attributes for each object type; not an implementation schema yet.
notes: Use this alongside documentation/object-types.md to keep terminology consistent.
Fields marked *_tbd are placeholders and should be resolved through design decisions.

notes_tbd: Define how to fast-forward systems whose rates change during catch-up (caps, gating, brownouts, etc.).
Define exact Vibes consumption per crew (and whether it scales).
Materials default to untuned persistence; override per material when needed.
Decision CL-0081: Water is a material pool and persists across Tunings.
Decision CL-0082: Skin is a persistent loot item.
v0: extremely low-level tutorial opponent ("a deaf blind old grumpy rat...").
Fixed loot: 1 Skin.
Decision HIT-0006/DB-0003/DB-0011: tune stats so the fight is effectively not losable and Hero hit rate feels ~60% (do not force 100% hit rules).
Decision DB-0005: Dweller Bane wound capacity is 3 Light Wounds (implemented as 1 MW of 3 LW).
Decision DB-0006/DB-0007/VLUNIT-0001: bites every 5s; payload is 1 LW + 1 Viral Load (= +0.01 viral_load_ratio).
Decision DB-0008: low wound + low viral pressure are the safety valves.
Charges are used for ammo-like skills/items (e.g., a bow); replenishment can be time/object/perk based.
Decision SK-0001/SK-0002: starting skill is Attack (no separate basic).
Decision SK-0004: skills can have hit/crit rating modifiers and optional charges.
Decision IT-0003: bandaids remove one Light Wound (one wound unit).
Decision IT-0004: healing items can be used anywhere.
Decision IT-0008: no consumables during forced return.
Decision IT-0011: Glue restores +3 wound units to one damaged gear item.
Decision IT-0012: player chooses which gear item is repaired.
v0: Base/Studio tile uses standard per-tile Investigate→Explore leveling.
Investigate can be done by Hero or crew; Explore is Hero-only.
Decision DB-0010: Dweller Bane victory triggers the 'cleaning impulse' event; it unlocks the Crystal Circle upgrade 'Removing Moss'.
Decision BU-0008 (refinement): 'accessible' for expeditions/out-of-base placement is: in bubble + Hero visited (and any authored access conditions for special content).
Decision EX-0001: unlocks are stored as a global set.
Decision EX-0012: unlock is granted by tile exploration AND the dungeon also stores a home_tile_id (for gating/UI consistency).
Decision EX-0012: dungeon stores home_tile_id.
Dungeon unlock is granted by tile exploration (ContentUnlockDefinition).
Resonance is recomputed on Tuning rather than accumulated.
Grace decision XP-0011: after the first class change, allow one additional switch within 10s (undo/adjust), then enforce cooldown.
Define exploration/combat stats progression separately.
Decisions TE-0001..TE-0010.
Used for point-of-no-return time-to-safety and for expedition/recruit travel-time computation.
Decisions MV-0001..MV-0010: plains base is 1s/tile; movement travel-time is separate from bubble impedance; travel progresses offline; travel can trigger events.
Decision GE-0011: belt count is 1; rings max is 10 (each finger), but fingers can be lost which reduces available ring slots.
Decision GE-0017: finger loss comes from Echo Scars and special events.
Decision GE-0012: use a rational weighted table (sums to 100%) rather than uniform random.
Decision GE-0013: at 0 durability, the item remains equipped but provides 0 wound protection.
Decision GE-0006: same pool; default 1 MW = 3 LW for gear unless item overrides via its Y.
Decision GE-0001..GE-0010.
Decision TE-0009: entering a fogged tile reveals it on the map, but Explore/Investigate still gate deeper content.
Example v0 table: mostly nothing, occasional loot.
Example v0 table: more combat/event pressure outside bubble; tune later.
Hot tile override table keyed off tile content tags.
Finger loss is triggered by some Echo Scars and special events (Decision GE-0017/ES-0014).
Grace decision XP-0011: after the first class change, allow one additional switch within 10s (undo/adjust), then enforce cooldown.
Fire Pit is special-cased to be max_count=1 (player-facing warning copy).
Fire Pit tuning affinity is untuned (persists across Tunings).
Early: only Drummer/Bassline is unlocked; other classes/bands unlock later.
Crew: multiplier uses the active class level only (for the produced band).
Hero: when producing a band, all class levels contribute as multiplicative multipliers.
Decision CL-0057: applies to all band outputs.
Decision CL-0064: time-only (10s), no Stone cost.
Decision CL-0067: this upgrade is untuned (persists across Tunings).
Decision BA-0036: becomes available after Dweller Bane death (story trigger).
Decision BA-0036: activates passive Bassline generation (+1/tick) as part of cleaning.
Decision CL-0058: modifies K_field (bubble budget conversion).
Decision CL-0066: start at +1% (K_field_mult=1.01).
Decision CL-0075: costs 1 skin + 5 water (liters), duration 4s.
Decision CL-0084: this upgrade is untuned (persists across Tunings).
Crystal Circle outputs are early-source of band generation; exact tick amounts TBD.

restoration_tbd.restore_upgrade_name_tbd: Restore public spaces of the ground floor
notes_tbd: Restore The Studio is an early objective; it is required (alongside Fire Pit + ReachFromBase>=3) before first recruitment.
This is a free starter structure and persists across Tunings.
v0: starts damaged with 0 bunks until restored.
This is a per-terrain-type template; actual tiles/coords live in authored map data files.
Stone nodes do not regenerate by default.
All terrain can carry ambient resources; nodes represent concentrated deposits.
World-state lives per save slot; this state is separate from the node definition.
Early decision: expeditions return loot/materials, not band resources directly.
Use per-system tick accumulators + event scheduling; stop at caps.
v0: passive Bassline trickle is unlocked by the Crystal Circle upgrade 'Removing Moss' to reduce dependence on constant staffing.

repeatable_rewards_rng_tbd.seed_derivation: seed64 = hash64(global_seed, 'exploration_loot', tile_id, exploration_level, reward_id, claim_count)

notes_tbd: Decisions CL-0111..CL-0120: exploration levels persist per tile per save slot; Investigate can be done by Hero or crew; Explore is Hero-only; no offline progression.
Decision CL-0116: unlock grants are one-time by default; some tiles/levels can be configured repeatable (random loot within parameters).
v0 only: base tile has Stone stock available so Restore The Studio + Fire Pit can be completed quickly.
Full scavenge system (tile stocks, diminishing, per-actor efficiency curves) is designed later.
Decision CL-0091/CL-0092/CL-0093: water starts at 5L on the Studio tile, max 30L, regenerates slowly, and is collected at 2L/s.
Decision CL-0105: water collection is available as soon as the player starts playing.
Used for worker-blocking construction/upgrade actions where partial progress may persist.
Used for build timers, recruit travel timers, expedition timers.
Timers are authoritative; an in-memory event queue is derived from these on load.
Determinism: simulation events advance counters; UI should not.
Stores fractional tick remainder so long offline sessions do not drift.
If under construction, construction_task_tbd is a WorkTaskState (worker-blocking, progress can persist).
Stations are treated like buildings for staffing, but may have different placement rules.
Stores per-tile entry counters for deterministic travel encounter seeding and any queued encounters when offline.
Grace decision XP-0011: after the first class change, allow one additional switch within 10s (undo/adjust).
Hero is unique; exploration/combat state will be added later.
Wounds are expressed as X Major Wounds made of Y Light Wounds.
v0 Hero baseline: 3 MW of 6 LW.
Same-pool model: Light Wound adds +1 wound unit; Major Wound adds +light_wounds_per_major wound units.
Derived UI: major_taken = floor(wound_units_taken / light_wounds_per_major), light_remainder = wound_units_taken % light_wounds_per_major.
v0: recruit travel can be cancelled if recruitment becomes disabled (ReachFromBase gate lost).
Instant stock behavior is defined in RecruitmentSystemConfig; this state only stores current values and in-flight recruits.
Expeditions always complete (no failure); progress and returns are subject to caps and destination locking rules.
Catch-up processes time in segments between events; see documentation/save-load.md.
Index file lists available slots and their names; state lives in each slot folder.
Design-time config for save cadence/retention; implementation may override per platform.

layer_cost_model.definition: ring_cost[k] = sum(terrain_impedance(tile) for tile in Ring(k) where eligible(tile)); cumulative_cost[r] = sum_{k<=r} ring_cost[k]
notes_tbd: Decision BU-0002/BU-0004: bubble expands/shrinks in full rings; the cost to expand is the summed impedance of all newly-conquered edge tiles.
K_field converts Bassline_field into an impedance budget for ripple layers; see mechanics/specifications.md and documentation/glossary.md.
Decision CL-0079: bunks do not hard-limit recruiting; they only drive BadVibes via overcapacity.
Decision MV-0009: recruit travel time uses the same movement/pathing system (sum of tile travel times).
Fallback: if pathing data is unavailable, approximate with distance_tiles * base_seconds_per_tile.
Decision CL-0089: losing reach (gate) cancels ongoing recruitments but still counts toward total recruited.

ratings.hit_chance: Let A=max(0,attacker_hit_rating), D=max(0,defender_hit_rating). Let A'=A*hero_advantage_mult if attacker is Hero else A. Let D'=D*hero_advantage_mult if defender is Hero else D. hit_chance = A'/(A'+D'); if A'=D'=0 => 0.5

crit_roll.crit_rating_total_tbd: crit_rating + theory_crit_bonus_percent + gear + perks + other_modifiers - malus

notes_tbd: Decision GE-0007: crit bypasses all gear durability for the whole attack.
Decision HIT-0014: crit bonus LW bypasses gear durability absorption and applies directly to the target's wound track.
Decision CRIT-0005/CRIT-0009: crit applies both the crit LW and the attack's normal Viral Load payload (not doubled).
Decision CRIT-0005: if the attack is fully absorbed by gear, Viral Load does not apply.
Decision HIT-0001/HIT-0002/HIT-0003: ratio model with d100; 3-tier outcomes; skills can miss and can carry hit buffs.
Decision HIT-0004/HIT-0008/HIT-0011: hit buffs/malus are rating adders; ratings are unbounded; hero advantage mult=1.15 (Hero-only).
Decision HIT-0010/HIT-0013: crit chance is driven by crit_rating and influenced by Theory (exact crit function TBD).
Decision HIT-0005/HIT-0014: crit adds +1 LW that ignores modifiers/blockers and bypasses gear durability.
Decisions CB-0001/CB-0005/CB-0006/CB-0007/CB-0008/CB-0009/CB-0010: cooldown combat; auto-fight unlocked later and cycles; travel encounters do not pause time; forced return used instead of death.

Decisions SK-0001..SK-0010: starting skill is Attack; skills can have charges; hero never applies viral load; victory condition is wound units with special events allowed; target tutorial clear ~30s; in-combat recovery is item-based.
Decision IT-0009: consumables persist across Tunings by default, but some can be tuned/run-scoped.
Decisions IT-0001..IT-0010.
Decision HIT-0011: hit/evade buffs are rating adders.
Hit/crit ratings are unbounded by default.
Used for band output, K_field, and other multiplicative scaling sources.
Decision MOD-0001: store both (implicit from owned/equipped sources + explicit active instances for temporary effects).
Decision MOD-0012: breakdown is recomputed; do not store snapshot breakdowns in saves.
Decision MOD-0010: use channels to simplify debugging and UI breakdowns.
Decision MOD-0007: Viral Load tiers do not feed into hit/crit/cooldown rating calculations in v0.
Per-definition policy controls re-application and stacking (Decision MOD-0002/MOD-0003).
Decision MOD-0009: modifier instances require stable IDs for determinism.
See documentation/modifier-system.md for the design overview.
Chorus starts locked at 0 early; this becomes relevant after Chorus unlock.
Resonance recompute math is in mechanics/specifications.md; encode here later once stabilized.
Decision VL-0008: viral load can be increased by events/conditions/encounters; no 'silent storms' concept.

failure_policy.device_lore: Survivors built a real-time Viral Load calculator; when you leave the bubble it logs the exit point and computes the Viral Load required to return to safety.
failure_policy.trigger_rule: If viral_load_ratio >= 1 - (required_time_to_reenter_bubble_seconds / hero_time_seconds_0_to_1), force auto-return (player cannot cancel).
failure_policy.required_time_to_reenter_bubble_seconds_: derived from movement system; recompute as needed while outside bubble.

notes_tbd: Recovery time is the inside-bubble recovery model; with linear recovery, time to full recovery is viral_load_ratio_at_arrival * time_seconds_1_to_0.
Lore phrasing: 'Viral Load points × 24s' can be interpreted as viral_load_ratio*10 points with 24s per point (=240s from 100%).
Decision VL-0011/VL-0012: forced auto-return once the 'point of no return' is crossed; full incapacitation; player cannot stop it.
Decisions VL-0001..VL-0010: single Viral Load scalar; progresses offline; rates anchored for Hero vs normal humans; recovery is slow.
Decision ES-0011/ES-0012: eligible pool can change by conditions; selection supports weighted random + deterministic filters + player choice.
Decision ES-0014/GE-0017: finger loss can be both a scar and a special event triggered by scars.
Example from ES-0018.
Net effect is tiny; mostly flavor unless expanded later.
Another tiny net-positive/negative pair; tune later to avoid triviality.
Decision ES-0001..ES-0006: Echo Scars exist in v0; triggered by forced-return; removable/mitigatable; uncapped stacks; affects Base and exploration.

theory_to_crit_bonus_percent_cumulative.result: theory_crit_bonus_percent = sum(add_crit_bonus_percent for milestones where Theory >= min_level)
notes_tbd: Decision ATTR-0004/ATTR-0007: Theory→crit% milestones are cumulative (sum of all reached milestones).
v0 default: 1 Tempo point = +1 hit-rating point (identity mapping).
If later balancing needs Tempo to matter more/less relative to gear/perks, adjust k_tempo_hit without changing the hit-chance ratio model.
Decisions ATTR-0001..ATTR-0003: attributes are unbounded, start at 0, and come from both progression and gear/perks.
